Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit expression of grape stilbene synthase in tomato enabled resveratrol production at ~0.5 mg g^-1 DW in the original 35S:VvStSy line. 2. Co-expression with AtMYB12 increased total resveratrol and derivatives to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW. 3. In AtMYB12 StSy fruit, high flavonol accumulation seen in AtMYB12 alone was reduced, indicating strong competition with chalcone synthase for phenylpropanoid flux.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS (competition for substrate inferred from pathway engineering)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolite profiling; RNA-seq GSE61014; ChIP-seq GSE62462
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduces flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 background by diverting flux toward stilbenes.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong accumulation of resveratrol and glycosylated/methylated resveratrol derivatives in tomato fruit, especially when combined with AtMYB12.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stilbene synthase; type III polyketide synthase
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; genetic crossing with AtMYB12 line; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural gene for tomato biofortification or production platforms; best deployed with AtMYB12 to boost precursor supply.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Original construct was 35S:VvStSy; paper emphasizes metabolite increase after crossing into E8:AtMYB12 background.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit harvested around 7–10 days after breaker
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive for VvStSy; combined with E8-driven AtMYB12 in crosses
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexin with reported therapeutic potential and lifespan-extension activity in model systems

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit-specific expression of Lotus japonicus isoflavone synthase in tomato produced low levels of genistin. 2. Addition of AtMYB12 significantly increased genistin to ~11 mg g^-1 DW. 3. Combining LjIFS with AtMYB12 and the are mutant background further increased total isoflavones to ~78 mg g^-1 DW by reducing competing F3H activity.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolite profiling; RNA-seq GSE61014; ChIP-seq GSE62462
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Isoflavone production depends on rerouting flux away from flavonol branch; competition with endogenous F3H affects output.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables and increases isoflavone accumulation in tomato fruit, especially with AtMYB12 and reduced F3H activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression under E8 promoter; crossing with AtMYB12 are mutant line; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering phytoestrogen-enriched tomato when combined with AtMYB12 and branch competition control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genistin was the only isoflavonoid detected in LjIFS-only fruit; much higher accumulation required both supply enhancement and branch rerouting.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit harvested around 7–10 days after breaker
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogenic isoflavone linked to dietary effects on hormone-related diseases

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The natural are mutant is deficient in F3H activity. 2. In AtMYB12 are-/- fruit, flavonol content fell to ~30% of AtMYB12 fruit. 3. When combined with LjIFS and AtMYB12, the are mutation greatly increased isoflavone accumulation by reducing competition for naringenin.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strongly decreases flavonol production while favoring isoflavone production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reroutes phenylpropanoid flux away from flavonols toward isoflavones in the engineered background.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutant locus causing reduced flavanone 3-hydroxylase activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crossing and segregation analysis; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful flux-rerouting allele to enhance isoflavone production in engineered tomato systems.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats are as a naturally occurring mutant background rather than cloning a specific gene sequence.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit harvested around 7–10 days after breaker

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope feeding/metabolic flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GSE61014; ChIP-seq GSE62462; metabolomics/isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for shikimate-derived aromatic amino acids and downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages around 3–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway / aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDAHPS transcripts were elevated in AtMYB12 fruit. 2. Its promoter was directly bound by AtMYB12 and by endogenous SlMYB12 in peel. 3. Activation of SlDAHPS is linked to increased aromatic amino acid supply and enhanced phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor supply for phenylpropanoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form DAHP, the entry metabolite of the shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a key gene linking primary metabolism to aromatic amino acid and secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported as a key determinant of carbon flow into the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in peel with SlMYB12 expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic phytonutrient production and associated health-promoting properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for increasing precursor supply to aromatic amino acid-derived metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter directly activated by AtMYB12/SlMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope feeding/metabolic flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GSE61014; ChIP-seq GSE62462; metabolomics/isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool by increasing phosphoenolpyruvate availability for shikimate-derived phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stages around 3–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlENO transcript abundance increased in AtMYB12 fruit. 2. AtMYB12 and endogenous SlMYB12 directly bound the SlENO promoter. 3. Activation of SlENO is associated with greater glycolytic flux supporting phenylpropanoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances carbon flux from primary metabolism toward aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper specifically emphasizes plastidial ENO as a direct AtMYB12 target connecting glycolysis to specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glycolytic precursor supply to shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key gene linking primary metabolism to specialized metabolism; explicit rate-limiting status not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; higher in peel where SlMYB12 is expressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic phytonutrient production and associated health-promoting properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply target to pair with downstream phenylpropanoid genes or regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter directly activated by AtMYB12/SlMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling in are mutant background; pathway competition analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR/heat map expression data
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with engineered isoflavone branch for shared substrate and supports flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit harvested around 7–10 days after breaker
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H is a known AtMYB12 target in flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. The are mutant background reduces F3H activity and lowers flavonol accumulation to ~30% of AtMYB12 fruit. 3. Reduced F3H activity increases isoflavone production when LjIFS is expressed, consistent with competition for naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol biosynthesis but limits engineered isoflavone accumulation by competing for substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to produce dihydroflavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural are mutant is deficient in F3H activity; this deficiency was exploited for branch rerouting.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol branch versus isoflavone branch competition point
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-competition control point in this engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in AtMYB12 fruit and peel
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing F3H activity lowers flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols and isoflavones are nutritionally relevant phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful editing target to reroute flux toward isoflavones when paired with IFS and AtMYB12.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates genes of primary metabolism and early/general phenylpropanoid/flavonoid biosynthesis, including direct activation of ENO, DAHPS, PAL5A, PAL5C, PAL5D, CHS1 and F3H
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive 35S-driven expression in tobacco previously reduced growth; E8 fruit-specific expression gives stronger fruit metabolic effects with less growth penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: flavonols; hydroxycinnamates; chlorogenic acid; anthocyanins; resveratrol derivatives; isoflavones
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB12 strongly increased expression of genes in glycolysis, the pentose phosphate pathway, the shikimate pathway, and flavonoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. ChIP-qPCR and ChIP-seq showed direct binding to promoters of ENO, DAHPS, PAL5A, PAL5C, PAL5D, CHS1 and F3H via a TACCTACC motif. 3. AtMYB12 reprogrammed carbon flux, increasing precursor, energy and reducing power supply for aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis. 4. Co-expression with Del/Ros1 or structural genes VvStSy and LjIFS enabled much higher anthocyanin, stilbene and isoflavone accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies AtMYB12 as the main multi-level engineering regulator controlling both supply and demand. It is the central core gene of the study.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlENO; SlDAHPS; PAL5A; PAL5C; PAL5D; CHS1; F3H
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic fruit-specific expression; RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope labeling/flux analysis; metabolite profiling; genetic crosses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master engineering regulator that coordinately increases precursor supply and phenylpropanoid pathway demand, giving exceptionally high metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value tool for biofortification and metabolic engineering; can be stacked with pathway genes or other TFs to produce high levels of desired phenylpropanoids in fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Precursor supply, aromatic amino acid biosynthesis, flavonoid/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, branch enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in tomato fruit under E8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; strongest induction around breaker to post-breaker stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes accumulation of diverse phenylpropanoid compounds by activating both primary and secondary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GSE61014; ChIP-seq GSE62462; LC-MS metabolomics; isotope tracing
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary polyphenols with health-promoting and antioxidant-related benefits

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds and likely activates SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 in peel, sharing targets with AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in fruit peel, not flesh
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous SlMYB12 is predominantly expressed in tomato peel. 2. SlMYB12 binds the same promoter motifs in SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 as AtMYB12. 3. Its expression is associated with elevated expression of primary and secondary metabolic genes in peel.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results provide direct experimental evidence for its shared target specificity and peel-associated regulation; however it is a supporting endogenous homologue rather than the primary engineering transgene.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunodetection; qRT-PCR in peel versus flesh; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Peel-associated endogenous regulator of flavonoid accumulation sharing AtMYB12 target genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous allele target for peel flavonoid enhancement, though the paper mainly exploits AtMYB12 transgenically.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Primary-metabolism support and flavonoid biosynthesis in peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly in peel at 5 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Peel versus flesh comparison; post-breaker fruit stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with elevated peel expression of genes supporting flavonol biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective/nutritional flavonoid accumulation in tomato peel

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Together with Ros1 mainly activates late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes rather than primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not enhance aromatic amino acid supply like AtMYB12; therefore lower breadth of phenylpropanoid enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del in combination with Ros1 activates anthocyanin biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. Unlike AtMYB12, Del/Ros1 does not substantially activate primary metabolic pathways or increase aromatic amino acids. 3. Combining Del/Ros1 with AtMYB12 in Indigo tomatoes further increases anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive engineering regulator in this study; acts mainly on anthocyanin demand side.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crosses; RT-qPCR heat map; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-specific activator of anthocyanin accumulation, but less broad than AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic expression in purple tomato line
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in stacking with AtMYB12 to increase anthocyanins plus copigmenting flavonols.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic fruit expression in purple line
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit, especially when stacked with AtMYB12.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Together with Del mainly activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes rather than primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not increase primary metabolic support or aromatic amino acid pools like AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ros1 together with Del drives anthocyanin biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly activates anthocyanin-pathway genes without notable activation of primary metabolism. 3. Stacking with AtMYB12 increases anthocyanin accumulation and produces the Indigo phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive regulator in the combinatorial engineering design.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crosses; RT-qPCR heat map; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts with Del in anthocyanin regulatory complex/network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Activator of anthocyanin production in purple tomato fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic expression in purple tomato line
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with AtMYB12 for high-anthocyanin fruit with additional flavonol copigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic fruit expression in purple line
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit, especially when combined with Del and AtMYB12.
